concerns.text: Spec FR-005 mandates running 'LALInference CPU-mode'. The tasks (T026, T026.1) implement 'Bilby/Dynesty' instead. While the Plan justifies this as a deviation, there is NO task to formally amend the Spec or Constitution to reflect this change before implementation. The tasks execute a requirement violation (using a different engine) without a corresponding task to update the governing spec artifacts, leaving the implementation unanchored to a valid requirement.

Spec FR-001 requires downloading '≥15 compact binary coalescence injection events from GWOSC API'. The Plan admits no such public injection campaign exists. Tasks T012-T016 implement 'synthetic injections' into real noise. There is no task to formally update Spec FR-001 to reflect this data source change. The tasks implement a solution to a non-existent requirement (downloading public injections) without a task to revise the requirement itself, creating a traceability gap.

Spec SC-003 and FR-010 require measuring bias against 'known true parameters from injection campaigns'. The Plan introduces an 'External Baseline' (Bias_Original) and measures 'Delta_Bias'. Tasks T028.1 and T028 implement loading this external baseline. There is no task to update Spec SC-003 or FR-010 to reflect the shift from absolute bias measurement to relative delta measurement. The tasks execute a changed success criterion without a task to update the spec.

Spec FR-003 requires applying 'JPEG2000' compression. Tasks T020/T020.1 implement '1D-to-2D folding' as a workaround. While the Plan notes this deviation, there is no task to formally record this deviation in a provenance file *before* the compression logic is written, as required by Constitution Principle VI. The task description mentions recording it, but the task itself is the implementation; a distinct task or explicit step to create the provenance record *prior* to execution is missing to satisfy the 'record deviations' constraint.

Task T027 implements a fallback to 'Paired t-tests/Wilcoxon' if the Hierarchical Bayesian test fails. Spec FR-007 mandates 'MUST perform hierarchical Bayesian shift tests'. The task description implies the fallback is acceptable, but there is no explicit task to update Spec FR-007 to authorize this fallback as a valid substitute for the primary requirement. The task assumes a spec amendment that hasn't been executed.
